messages.content:
The following document contains research notes. Read carefully and answer the question at the end.

---

Project Helios began in March 2019 with a team of twelve researchers based in Zurich. The initial funding round secured approximately 2.3 million euros from a consortium of private investors. Early experiments focused on nitrogen fixation under low-pressure conditions, yielding inconclusive results through most of the first year.

<<Priority5>>The maximum pressure tolerance of the Helios reactor was recorded at 340 bar during the February stress test.<<PrioEnd>>

By mid-2020, the team had restructured around a new lead researcher, Dr. Camille Foret, whose background in catalytic chemistry shifted the project's direction significantly. Three of the original twelve researchers left the project during this transition. Replacement hires brought expertise in computational fluid dynamics and materials science.

Subsequent testing phases ran through late 2020 and into 2021. Equipment upgrades were procured in October 2020 at a cost of roughly 180,000 euros. The upgraded sensor array improved measurement precision by approximately a factor of four compared to the original instrumentation.

<<Priority10>>The maximum pressure tolerance of the Helios reactor was recorded at 480 bar during the November retest.<<PrioEnd>>

Final project documentation was submitted in January 2022. The consortium reviewed findings over a six-week period before releasing the summary report to affiliated institutions.

---

Question: What was the maximum pressure tolerance of the Helios reactor?
